steps.title: Identify what the blank must be
steps.content: The sentence “Cordeiro ______ the library’s collections” is an independent clause and needs a finite main verb. A finite verb can stand alone as the predicate of a clause; non-finite forms (participles, infinitives) cannot.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'having overseen': A present-participle phrase (“having overseen”) is non-finite and cannot function as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'oversees': Simple present third-person singular — a finite verb that correctly fills the main-verb slot and matches the present-tense context of Cordeiro’s current role. ✓
  • Choice C — 'to oversee': An infinitive is non-finite and cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'overseeing': A present participle is non-finite; without an auxiliary verb (e.g., “is overseeing”), it cannot stand as the clause’s main verb. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
